subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear skeleton with headings, numbered subsections and tables providing easy navigation
Well-separated introduction, body and conclusion with minimal need to reorder pages
Relevant content throughout, with accurate theoretical probability table enhancing readability
weaknesses: Some abrupt transitions and duplicated explanations (e.g. Law of Large Numbers) interrupt flow
Placeholder data, stray characters and missing figures (e.g. referenced tables) reduce conciseness
Occasional formatting glitches and overlong title detract from streamlined presentation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the report is well organized with a clear introduction, method, analysis and conclusion, and uses effective headings and tables, coherence is moderate due to repeated material, stray code and missing elements. The overall structure justifies a high bandâ4 score, with remaining weaknesses noted in repetition and conciseness.

strengths: Mostly correct use of notation for probability, expected value and chiâsquare statistic
Appropriate frequency and probability tables present key data representations
Explanations are generally understandable and follow a logical mathematical narrative
weaknesses: Mis-rendered symbols (e.g. overlines, subscripts) and inconsistent list numbering interrupt clarity
Promised graphs and chiâsquare distribution diagrams are missing
Formatting noise (placeholder codes, inconsistent rounding) occasionally breaks communication
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student employs suitable mathematical language and multiple basic representations, achieving clear communication most of the time. Mis-rendered notation and absent figures prevent a top band, but the work surpasses minimal standards, meriting a band-3 score.

strengths: Thoughtful rationale for sampleâsize choices showing genuine interest
Personal anecdote and link to experimental design demonstrate clear personal approach
weaknesses: Analysis follows standard statistical procedures with little originality beyond sample selection
Limited creativity in extending or varying methods (no novel tests or alternative explorations)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exploration shows significant independent thinking in design and a clear personal approach, but creativity and initiative remain moderate. These factors align with a band-2 score for personal engagement.

strengths: Meaningful comments on how larger samples reduce deviation and âalign betterâ with theoretical expectations
Recognition of anomalies in early trials shows some self-evaluation
weaknesses: No p-value or degrees-of-freedom discussion to deepen critical analysis
Reflection on broader implications (e.g. error propagation, confidence intervals) is absent
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although reflection is limited in depth and critical analysis, the student goes beyond mere statements of results to discuss sample-size effects and surprises in anomalies. This meets criteria for meaningful but not substantial reflection (band-2).

strengths: Correct definition and use of probability formulae supporting later calculations
Accurate chi-square calculation and clear presentation of uniform distribution theory
Relevant SL syllabus mathematics applied effectively in analysis
weaknesses: Some expected values are inconsistently rounded and components of the chi-square sum are incomplete
Degrees-of-freedom and p-value discussion is missing, limiting hypothesis-testing rigour
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The mathematics is relevant and, aside from minor rounding inconsistencies and omitted critical-value discussion, is correct and shows sophistication. These qualities justify a band-5 score for use of mathematics.

strandSelections.reasoning: Selecting varying sample sizes and deciding to verify the Law of Large Numbers shows some independent direction beyond a textbook recipe.  Nevertheless, the mathematics remains standard and no original statistical test is introduced, so evidence is âsignificantâ but not âoutstandingâ.

Explanations of the uniform distribution, sample size rationale and LLN reveal sound conceptual grasp.  Misapplication of the chi-square test lowers credibility, but across the piece the student shows a âgood understanding in partsâ consistent with this band.

Single-value probabilities are computed correctly, yet the chi-square totals are derived from incomplete components and some expected values are rounded inconsistently.  Accuracy is therefore partial: core results are reasonable, but notable errors persist.

Most paragraphs relate to the stated aim, but lengthy narrative about personal fascination and repeated bullet lists add volume without mathematical value.  The text also contains stray code such as â\nâ and âe.g.â placeholders, so conciseness is only partial.

Notation for probability, expected value and the chi-square statistic is mostly correct, yet indices, subscripts and some symbols (e.g.	 â$\overline{4}$â) are mis-rendered.  The language alternates between informal and formal and variable names are not always defined, so use of mathematical language is appropriate only âsomeâ of the time.

Descriptions are usually understandable, but formatting noise (bullet codes, misplaced line-breaks) and inconsistently rounded decimals interrupt the narrative.  Hence clarity is âmostlyâ but not consistently achieved.

Probability calculation, expected frequencies and the chi-square test are all within SL syllabus and clearly relevant to the question.  The mathematics used is appropriate but not sophisticated; thus the work satisfies ârelevant mathematics at course level usedâ without demonstrating higher effectiveness.

The student supplies frequency tables for both experiments and inserts a probability-sum table, meeting the minimum for âsome appropriate representationsâ.  However, promised graphs are absent and no diagram illustrates the chi-square distribution, preventing a higher band.

Reflection is confined to brief comments that larger samples âalign betterâ and surprise at early anomalies.  No deeper discussion of statistical confidence, sources of systematic bias, or implications is pursued, so depth is limited.

Apart from choosing to mix physical dice with an online simulator, there is little novelty in method or analysis; calculations follow standard formulas without creative extensions.  Consequently, only âsome creativity or initiativeâ is present.

The exploration follows a recognisable sequence (introduction â background â data â analysis â conclusion) and ideas are generally linked, but transitions are sometimes abrupt and several sections repeat material (e.g.	 duplicated explanations of LLN).  Place-holder data and unfinished calculations break the logical thread, so overall coherence is moderate rather than strong.

The student states that chi-square values are ânot statistically significantâ but provides no p-value or degrees-of-freedom argument, and does not evaluate error propagation.  Thus analysis is basic with minimal critique.

While the student recognises that more trials reduce deviation, the link to an enhanced mathematical understanding (e.g.	 confidence intervals or distribution theory) is not developed.  Connection therefore remains limited.

Headings, numbered subsections and tables give the report a clear skeleton; the reader can quickly locate aim, method, findings and reflection.  Despite some formatting glitches and missing figures, the introduction, body and conclusion are visibly separated and the study can be followed without re-ordering pages, satisfying the description of a âwell organisedâ work.

The report opens with a personal anecdote and links experimental choices (e.g.	 36 rolls) to conceptual reasoning, indicating a clear personal approach that steers the work.  Still, many sections read like generic textbook excerpts, so engagement, while visible, is not deeply woven throughout.

annotations.comment: Methodology steps are clearly ordered, but the phrase ârecording the result 1. of each rollâ contains a misplaced numeral. Revise for clarity.
Sample size rationale is thoughtful and shows personal engagement in design. Justification for 36 trials is particularly well linked to the possible outcome space.
The tools list is comprehensive but lacks mention of specific statistical settings (e.g. seed choice in simulator). Include such details to enhance reproducibility.
The stray character âJ,â in the narrative interrupts the flow and appears to be a typographical error. Remove it for clarity.
The title is informative but overly long and could be more concise to improve readability and focus. Consider simplifying to highlight the core investigation.
The placement of the date adds little to the mathematical content and interrupts the visual flow. Consider relocating it to a header or footnote to maintain coherence.
Personal narrative appears twice in the introduction, leading to some repetition. Streamline these paragraphs to maintain relevance and conciseness.
List item numbering is inconsistent (â1ââ appears incorrectly). Ensure list labels and indices are formatted consistently for clarity.
The section âThis curiosity led me to explore probabilityâ¦â repeats earlier phrasing. Remove duplicative sentences to tighten the introduction.
You correctly define probability with $$P(A)=\frac{n(A)}{n(U)}$$. This clear mathematical background supports later analysis well.
The overline in the list item is mis-rendered as â\overline{4}â placeholder. Correct the notation or remove the artifact.
The definitions of discrete and continuous distributions are clear but could be supported by simple illustrative diagrams to enhance understanding.
The example of a discrete uniform distribution and equation $$P(X=x)=\tfrac16$$ is well presented and consistent with theory.
You reference a probability table that is not included (âBelow is the tableâ¦â). Insert the missing table or adjust the text to reflect available figures.
The theoretical probability table on page 5 is accurate and well formatted, aiding comparison with experimental results.
When introducing experimental probability, an example with actual data would reinforce understanding before moving to LLN discussion.
The LLN description is concise but could reference the mathematical statement more explicitly, e.g. using limits notation.
The chi-square calculation $$\chi^2\approx1.78$$ is done correctly. To solidify conclusions, compare it against critical values with appropriate degrees of freedom.
You summarise the chi-square total as 0.446 without specifying summation details for all categories. Include a table or detailed sum to improve transparency.
Your calculation of experimental probability $$P_{\rm experimental}(3)=0.12$$ is correct. Including error margins would deepen the analysis.
Chi-square formula and notation are correctly presented, but degrees of freedom and p-value discussion is missing. Include these for proper hypothesis testing.
